reasoning_content: We need to determine if 263 is prime. Check divisibility by small primes: 2: no, 3: sum digits 2+6+3=11 not divisible by 3, 5: no, 7: 7*37=259, 7*38=266, so not divisible, 11: 11*23=253, 11*24=264, so no, 13: 13*20=260, 13*21=273, so no, 17: 17*15=255, 17*16=272, so no, 19: 19*13=247, 19*14=266, so no, 23: 23*11=253, 23*12=276, so no, 29: 29*9=261, 29*10=290, so no, 31: 31*8=248, 31*9=279, so no. Next prime sqrt(263) is about 16.2, so we only need to check primes up to 13? Actually sqrt(263) ~ 16.2, so primes up to 13 are sufficient because 17^2=289>263, so we only need to check primes ≤13. Already did 2,3,5,7,11,13. None divide. So 263 is prime. Answer yes.
